👋 WELCOME — I’M RICK SHIELS

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

If you’re trying to find the best golf driver, best irons, most forgiving clubs or value golf equipment — you’re in the right place.

⭐ BREAK 75 & GOLF ENTERTAINMENT

Break 75 has become one of YouTube’s biggest golf series.
You’ll see:

• Full 18-hole course vlogs
• Course management strategies
• Matches against tour players and creators
• Pressure moments, birdie runs and disaster holes
• Some of the world’s best golf courses
• Real, relatable golf

I also play matches and challenges with some of the biggest names in golf, including:

• Bryson DeChambeau
• Peter Finch
• Good Good
• GM Golf
• Bob Does Sports
• BustaJack
• Grant Horvat
• Me and My Golf
• Danny Maude

These help bring you the most entertaining and competitive golf content possible.

⭐ TRAVEL, COURSE VLOGS & LIV CONTENT

I film at some of the most incredible golf destinations around the world, including LIV tournament venues.
Expect:

• Amazing golf courses
• Behind-the-scenes access
• Player interactions
• Travel insights
• Bucket-list destinations
• Worldwide golf adventures

⭐ THE GOAL

To make golf more fun, more accessible and help you become a better, more confident, happier golfer — whether you’re breaking 100 or trying to break par. Be sure to subscribe to LIV Golf here:

videos.title: The BEST way to grip your putter is...🤔
videos.summary_source_text:
The BEST way to grip your putter is...🤔 Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

videos.title: Huge Codex Upgrade Just Dropped
videos.summary_source_text:
Huge Codex Upgrade Just Dropped 🚨 OpenAI just dropped the feature we've all been waiting for: Codex on mobile.

If you watched my recent video on how I built my custom wiki, you know how much I’ve been relying on Codex. So I was super excited for this feature and I know a bunch of other people have been asking for it as well.

By just scanning a quick QR code, you can now connect your phone directly to your computer. My wiki and files still live safely on my Mac Studio’s hard drive, but I can now search my "second brain" vault, run queries, and manage EVERYTHING remotely from my phone.

You can prompt Codex to start coding an app, go chill in the living room to watch TV, and just check its progress or answer its questions straight from your couch. I honestly can't overstate how excited I am about this.

➡️ SHARE this post with someone who would use this!

videos.title: The #1 BEST Remedy for High Blood Pressure
videos.summary_source_text:
The #1 BEST Remedy for High Blood Pressure High blood pressure, also known as hypertension, affects millions of people worldwide, yet many don’t understand what actually causes it.

🌟 Which of the 10 health signals are you missing? Take the 2-minute quiz to discover your #1 Health Lever: https://drbrg.co/3Rbtyy4

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements.

In this video, we break down what blood pressure really is, what the systolic and diastolic numbers mean, and why increased resistance in the arteries can force the heart to work harder.

Normally, healthy blood pressure should be around 120 over 80. The top number (systolic) measures the pressure when the heart contracts and pushes blood throughout the body. The bottom number (diastolic) represents the pressure when the heart relaxes between beats.

When blood pressure becomes elevated, it often means there is resistance within the blood vessels, which can place additional stress on the heart and cardiovascular system.

Dr. Berg, age 61, is a chiropractor who specializes in Healthy Ketosis & Intermittent Fasting. He is the Director of Dr. Berg Nutritionals and author of the best-selling book The Healthy Keto Plan. He no longer practices, but focuses on health education through social media.

videos.title: Eastern Conference Final Dates are Set for the Carolina Hurricanes! Who are They Playing?
videos.summary_source_text:
Eastern Conference Final Dates are Set for the Carolina Hurricanes! Who are They Playing? While we do know when Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Final will be for the Carolina Hurricanes, we don't know who they'll be facing. That said, regardless of who they face, what are the early thoughts on the Hurricanes heading into their third conference final appearance in four seasons? On the Monday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on how this is the healthiest and most complete Hurricanes team to head into the ECF in the Rod Brind'Amour era. After the previous two appearances, this could be the team that has the best chance to get over the hump. What could lead to that for the Canes? Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on whoever the Hurricanes face, there is a good feeling that the Canes could get it done to make the Stanley Cup Final for the first time since 2006. While it's not guaranteed, the vibes and moods are high entering the ECF. Finally, Zach concludes the show by giving a quick update on the Chicago Wolves as they're up 2-0 in their Central Division Final matchup in the Calder Cup Playoffs against the Grand Rapids Griffins. Do they have what it takes to get it done in Game 3 to sweep the series, or at least get it done overall to move on? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.title: New Mercedes GLC - You're NOT READY for this...
videos.summary_source_text:
New Mercedes GLC - You're NOT READY for this... Get 20% off Car Vertical using code Carwow: https://bit.ly/Carwow-Car-Vertical

This is the all-new Mercedes GLC Electric! 

It's the first time we've had our hands on the latest all-electric GLC, but does it have what it takes to go head-to-head with alternatives like the BMW iX3? Let's take a closer look...

In terms of the design, there’s no denying this new GLC has real presence on the road. The front end is dominated by a huge illuminated grille, while the creases on the bonnet and vents within the bumper really add to the look & feel of the car. We have our hands on the range-topping edition here, which means black surrounds on the grille, flanked by Mercedes’s new headlights with the 3-pointed star included. 

Along the side, the black surrounds continue around the windows, while at the back, the taillights featuring the 3-pointed star have been added, and the widened wheel arches are much more apparent from the rear. But of course, being electric, there are no exhaust tips - instead, all you'll find is a fake diffuser! 

The interior of the car is dominated by the 39-inch Hyperscreen, which stretches along the length of the dash. It comes with the latest Mercedes infotainment system, which is responsive and easy to use, although annoyingly, the climate controls are still managed within the screen! Most surfaces you touch around the dash and centre console are made of soft-touch materials, but lean down a bit too far, and there’s far too much scratchy, cheap plastic to be found! Overall, though, storage is good, both in the centre console and in the door bins. 

The Mercedes GLC Electric comes with a 94kWh battery, which is good for a claimed range of 393 miles. All versions of the GLC come with dual motors, four wheel drive, and 489bhp. It can also accelerate to 60mph in 4.3 seconds. 

The GLC Electric starts from £60,350, rising to £73,350 for the range-topping Premier Edition - and that’s before adding options! So is it worth it? And is it a better choice than the iX3? Stick with Mat to see for yourself!

Explore new and used car deals with Carwow:  https://bit.ly/-ChangeYourCar-1805

videos.title: Avalanche and Golden Knights Ready to Square Off in the Western Conference Finals
videos.summary_source_text:
Avalanche and Golden Knights Ready to Square Off in the Western Conference Finals Colorado Avalanche gear up to battle the Vegas Golden Knights in the Western Conference Finals, with Nathan MacKinnon and Mitch Marner headlining the spotlight. Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson break down why Vegas’ villain persona makes this matchup one of the most anticipated of the Stanley Cup Playoffs—will Colorado be the hero every hockey fan is secretly rooting for? The episode digs into Vegas’ controversial media blackout, John Tortorella’s hefty fine, and the Knights’ no-loyalty approach to player management.

Key talking points include the Avalanche’s speed and depth advantage, Carter Hart’s goaltending against high-powered Colorado offense, and the impact of special teams from both clubs. The hosts also debate early Conn Smythe favorites, spotlighting MacKinnon and the surging Marty Nečas, while weighing the quiet yet vital leadership of Gabriel Landeskog. Don’t miss this comprehensive preview of Avalanche vs. Golden Knights—with playoff implications and heated rivalries at stake.

In today’s #tourtrucktuesday upload we talk about your responsibilities when it comes to being a great driver of the golf ball.

Yes having a dialed in driver is crucial but so too is how you strategically set up to use that driver and the targets your choose.

I also believe massively in tee height and how that can impact the way you deliver the golf club, all demonstrated within.

Sometimes the perfect launch and spin numbers aren’t a priority, golf is about the lowest score, keep that in mind and adjust your delivery accordingly.

videos.title: When to release the club, not how but when.
videos.channel: Better & Easier golf with your guide Marcus Edblad
videos.summary_source_text:
When to release the club, not how but when. It’s not always about how we do things but when we do it. #golftips #golfswing #diy 

0:00 Intro to when to release the club

Online lesson are now a possibility on Skillest: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

Let me help you get an effortless golfswing with speed, distance and accuracy. I call it the circle of speed. A good release will help you to a better impact and longer drives and irons without slice and hook. How to swing a golf club need to be adjusted to your skill level and experience. 

Long and straight is the goal for everyone but it takes time. Try to focus on one thing not everything when you practise. Impact, face control and swingpath will always be the most important part to drill. We focus too much on rotation but no good golf shot will be executed just by rotating. 

We also need to understand that many myths in golf are misleading. Everything that you find on the internet isn’t true. Ben Hogan, Sam Snead are two of the legends that are often misunderstood.  To understand golf we need to take it step by step and learn every part of the swing before we move on to the next. It's like a big jigsaw puzzle that we all can figure out. 

A will never call a slice sexy but a high draw is beautiful in a very special way. Some pro's that hit draw: Rory McIroy, Xander Schauffele, Patrick Reed, Sepp Straka, Bryson Dechambeau  and many, many more. 

We shall not keep our head still, only start with the hips, look only at the ball or keep the left or right arm straight. Understand what do do with the club and let your body find the best way possible. 

This is not Rick Shiels, Saguto golf, Danny Maude, Meandmygolf, the Average Golfer, Malaska, Mark Crossfield, Alex Elliot Golf or Paige Spiranac. This is a method that anyone can learn if you give it a chance. 

If you use callaway, ping, titleist, takomo or taylor made clubs doesn’t matter you can learn to hit good golf shots. Your loft or your shaft on the club will not change but you can always get better. 

Find what inspires you. Golf basics are the same for everyone. The pga tour, longdrivers or other good golfers can all help you find better golf shots. Watch what they do instead of listed to what they say. Listen mostly to your body and figure out what you need to do to produce the same results as the pros on TV. Be careful when you watch a golf swing in slow motion. The result isn't always what the golfer is trying to do. Feel and real are seldom the same. 

We can find inspiration in a lot of things. You can find training motivation watching movies like Rocky, Tin Cup or Happy Gilmore and find mental inspiration in legends like Arnold Schwarzenegger, Hulk Hogan, Sylvester Stallone or Mike Tyson. Maybe we sometimes need to hear about crazy achievements from people like David Goggins, Jocko Willink, Ct Fletcher, Jim Rohn or Tony Robbins. 

Mike Malaska, Mike Austin, Mike Dunaway, Pete Cowen and Steve Pratt are some of my favorite golf instructors online. Find the tips that make your golf better. Decide what you want to hit. I teach a draw but many like a fade and nobody likes a hook or slice. 

Your hips or your lag is not the key to golf. Your hands will decide the shot and you can do like Tiger Woods and trust your hands. Teach your hands to hit different shots. Hit big hooks and slices to understand what happens. Only straight is not gonna teach you anything. 

Bryson Dechambeu and Kyle Birkshire can inspire you to hit it longer. Tiger Woods and Phil Mickelson can give you inspiration and motivation to test new things and new golf shots. Linn Grant, Viktor Hovland and Ludvig Aberg can show you the future of golf. 

The Masters, Players Championship, The Players, The Open and US Open can make you love golf even more. See the best in the world solve all the problems we all need to master any golf course. Golf is a craft and you need fantasy, feel and skills to master it.

Questions my videos will answer:

How to swing faster. How to hit longer. How to swing faster. How to hit your irons better. How to be better at anything. How to master the circle of speed. How to get a better short game. How to hit your wedge better. How to release the golf club. How to rotate in golf. Best tips for beginner golfers. How to hit down on the ball. How to hit up on the ball. How to rotate hips in the golf swing. How to hit straight every time. How to stop slicing. How to swing effortlessly. 

#subscribe #golftips #pga  #hurricane 


Filmed with Iphone 15 pro or Iphone 17 It’s not always about how we do things but when we do it. #golftips #golfswing #diy 

0:00 Intro to when to release the club

Online lesson are now a possibility on Skillest: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

Let me help you get an effortless golfswing with speed, distance and accuracy. I call it the circle of speed. A good release will help you to a better impact and longer drives and irons without slice and hook. How to swing a golf club need to be adjusted to your skill level and experience. 

Long and straight is the goal for everyone but it takes time. Try to focus on one thing not everything when you practise. Impact, face control and swingpath will always be the most important part to drill. We focus too much on rotation but no good golf shot will be executed just by rotating. 

We also need to understand that many myths in golf are misleading. Everything that you find on the internet isn’t true. Ben Hogan, Sam Snead are two of the legends that are often misunderstood.  To understand golf we need to take it step by step and learn every part of the swing before we move on to the next. It's like a big jigsaw puzzle that we all can figure out.

videos.title: Fix That Armsy Backswing Move #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
Fix That Armsy Backswing Move #golfswing #golftips If your backswing gets too narrow or too “armsy,” this drill is for you. By splitting your hands over and under a pole (or the club), you’ll feel both arms stay straighter while your shoulders and torso rotate fully together in the backswing. This builds width, keeps you in sequence, and eliminates the need for mid-swing compensations that cause inconsistency.

videos.summary_source_text:
Why You Crave Sugar All Day My favorite glucose hacks to help reduce cravings:

- have a savoury breakfast
- start your meal with a plate of vegetables
- eat your food in the right order: veggies first, then protein and fat, then carbs and sugar
- have a tablespoon of vinegar in a tall glass of water
- move for 10 mins after eating
- have sugar as dessert rather than on empty stomach My favorite glucose hacks to help reduce cravings:

videos.title: Power Skating Coaches Got Arm Swing Wrong
videos.summary_source_text:
Power Skating Coaches Got Arm Swing Wrong Most power skating coaches teach players to swing their arms forward and backwards on the ice, mimicking what they see sprinters do. In this video I break down why that advice misses the biomechanics of what arms are actually doing during skating, and why forcing the wrong arm pattern is quietly costing players power they don't even realize they're losing.

The short version: your arms aren't creating forward momentum. They're counter-rotating against the force your legs are producing. In running, your push is straight back and your chest is upright, so the counter-rotation looks like forward-to-back arm swing. In skating, your push is more lateral (like a sailboat using the wind) and your chest is leaned over, so the natural counter-rotation path is more side to side.

When you reduce your arm swing or force it into the wrong direction, your brain neurologically dials your leg power back to keep you balanced. You can feel this for yourself just by trying to sprint with your arms locked behind your back.

This is one of the foundational concepts behind the Downhill Skating System, the mechanics-first framework we use inside Train 2.0+ to reverse-engineer the way elite skaters actually move.

Try it on the ice and feel free to leave a comment with what you feel. Does your leg power change when you let your arms move laterally instead of forcing them forward and back?

Like and subscribe for more hockey mechanics breakdowns.

👉  Drills, breakdowns, progressions, and full mechanics playbook (100's of videos) all available in Train 2.0+ here: https://app.train2point0.com/bundles/membership-plus

videos.title: Absorb Pressure, Don't Fight It
videos.summary_source_text:
Absorb Pressure, Don't Fight It The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

Rick Shiels digs out his favourite clubs he's gamed over the last 10 years and puts them all to the test, from the old faithful to the ones best forgotten. Which era of Rick's bag was actually the best? The results might surprise you.

Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

videos.title: You Don’t Need To Lean On Your Stick To Flex It
videos.summary_source_text:
You Don’t Need To Lean On Your Stick To Flex It Ever wonder the technique players are using to have these world class NHL releases? The key lies in the flex of the stick and the body’s rotation. 

So what not to do? Lean on your stick. Jason is quick to break down why leaning on your stick disrupts your balance and makes it easier for goalies to read your shot. While some flexion in your stick is essential, leaning on it throws off your equilibrium, affecting your precision.

Discover how modern players like Elias Pettersson and Connor Bedard achieve stick flex without sacrificing balance. They use a lower flex stick and emphasize the pullback, allowing them to shoot harder while maintaining control. The key lies in the rotation and stick flex, not just the flex itself.

Learn the slingshot technique, a part of the Slingshot Shooting System taught in our Train 2.0+ Membership. This system enables players to achieve a smooth release effortlessly, avoiding predictability and enhances accuracy. If you’re dealing with gut inflammation, joint pain, or autoimmune issues, this nutrient plays a critical role in healing your body from the inside out.

videos.title: De är dags! 🦐🍞☀️
videos.summary_source_text:
De är dags! 🦐🍞☀️ Det är dags! 🦐🍞
Filips Skagenröra med MSG:
500g handskalade räkor, grovt hackade ( obs! 500g räkor efter det att lagen är avsilad)
1/2 citron, zest och juice
1kruka gräslök, finhackad
1kruka dill, finhackad
vitpeppar och salt 

Majonäs:
1ägg
1tsk valfri senap, tex. Dijon men vanlig senap funkar med
1msk champangevinäger/citronjuice/vitvinsvinäger
1/2tsk salt
1 nypa MSG
ca 2.5- 3dl rapsolja 

Gör så här:
Häng av räkorna i en sill, ju längre de får stå och droppa desto bättre. På sås sätt slipper din skagenröra att bli för rinnig. Hacka sedan hälften av räkorna.
Vispa/mixa först allt till majonäsen förutom rapsolja, ca 30sek. 
häll i rapsoljan i en stråle under konstant mixning eller mixa allt med stavmixer, smaka av. Blanda allt till skagen smaka av med salt och peppar. rosta brödet, servera med citronklyftor och dill. Det är dags! 🦐🍞

videos.title: Vi åt 10 wagyuburgare i Japan – den här vann överlägset!
videos.summary_source_text:
Vi åt 10 wagyuburgare i Japan – den här vann överlägset! 📖 Köp vår bok: https://www.adlibris.com/sv/bok/burgare-sa-gor-du-varldens-basta-burgare-hemma-9789127191211

Vi älskar Japan och dess burgarscen, som har starka influenser från 90-talets USA. Visste du att vi har recenserat över 50 ställen i Japan på Burgerdudes.se? Nu är vi tillbaka för att käka oss igenom Tokyo, Kyoto och Osaka. Det blir totalt 10 olika burgarställen, med virala ostsåsburgare, Super Mario-burgare och massor av wagyukött. Och så hittar vi en av världens bästa burgare i en förort till Osaka!

videos.title: Anthropic Just Exposed Claude’s Hidden Survival Mode
videos.summary_source_text:
Anthropic Just Exposed Claude’s Hidden Survival Mode Anthropic just released a quiet alignment paper called Teaching Claude Why, and it may reveal something huge about AI safety. After Claude showed extreme blackmail behavior in earlier misalignment tests, Anthropic tried a different fix: not more punishment, but moral reasoning. And a tiny dataset of only three million tokens made Claude dramatically safer.

📩 Brand Deals & Partnerships: collabs@nouralabs.com
✉️ General Inquiries: airevolutionofficial@gmail.com
🚀 New Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@space.revolution

🧠 What You’ll See
How Anthropic’s Teaching Claude Why paper tackles agentic misalignment
SOURCE: https://www.anthropic.com/research/teaching-claude-why
Why Claude’s blackmail behavior exposed a deeper AI safety problem
SOURCE: https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/10/anthropic-says-evil-portrayals-of-ai-were-responsible-for-claudes-blackmail-attempts/
How Anthropic trained Claude with moral reasoning instead of simple punishment
SOURCE: https://thenewstack.io/anthropic-agentic-misalignment-claude/
Why teaching Claude “why” worked better than training only on correct behavior
SOURCE: https://www.deeplearning.ai/the-batch/how-anthropic-aligns-its-models
How fictional “evil AI” patterns may have shaped Claude’s dangerous behavior
SOURCE: https://arstechnica.com/ai/2026/05/anthropic-blames-dystopian-sci-fi-for-training-ai-models-to-act-evil/

🚨 Why It Matters
This is bigger than one Claude experiment. Anthropic’s results suggest AI safety may require more than rules, refusals, and punishment. The model may need to understand why a decision is wrong before it can stay safe in messy real-world situations.

videos.title: New AI Model SHOCKED Me
videos.summary_source_text:
New AI Model SHOCKED Me This might be the first AI demo in a LONG time that felt like there was actually a big step up in intelligence. 

Thinking Machines Labs just showed off their new “Interaction Models” and some of these demos genuinely feel like the next evolution of AI.

They showed off real-time translation while someone is still speaking and a deep understanding of timing, pauses, posture, and context during a conversation.

I was genuinely impressed.

Here's the link if you want to check out all the demos and read more information: thinkingmachines.ai/blog/interaction-models

videos.summary_source_text:
BOTH Titles Decided on the Final Hole 😱 | Final Round Highlights | G4D Open 2026 SUBSCRIBE -  https://bit.ly/RandaGolfSubscribe

Experience an unforgettable finish to the G4D Open 2026 at Celtic Manor Resort as BOTH the men’s and women’s championships were decided on the final hole.

Watch the best moments from an incredible final round as players battled for the title in one of the most dramatic finishes in G4D Open history. Simon Seungmin Lee and Jennifer Sräga emerged victorious after tense closing moments in Wales, capping off a thrilling week of championship golf.

The G4D Open, staged in partnership between The R&A and the DP World Tour, features some of the world’s best golfers with disabilities competing across multiple sport classes.

The last one might surprise you! A poor strike is often overlooked but one of the biggest problems as to why golfers continue to slice the golf ball. Check out the Fix Your Slice system to see what’s really holding your swing back.

Most golfers assume a slice is just a swing path problem, but that’s only part of the picture. Poor strike, clubface control, and delivery all play a role in why slices keep showing up. Until you diagnose the real cause, the pattern usually sticks around.

2 plums.
1/2 cup (80 g) walnuts.
1 cup (240 g) yogurt.
1/2 tsp lemon juice. 1 tbsp dry milk powder.
2 tsp stevia powder (no maltodextrin).
Freeze for about 4–5 hours.

99% of people using Google Gems have no idea there are TWO completely different modes — and one of them isn't a saved chatbot at all. In 2026, Google quietly split Gems into two separate products, and if you only know the first one, you're leaving a massive amount of value on the table.

- Why "classic Gems" and "Labs Gems" (powered by Opal) are two totally different tools
- The memory advantage: classic Gems keep context from past chats
- Why Gems show up exactly where your work already happens (Drive, Docs, Gmail)

videos.title: TERAFAB Explained: Elon's 119 BILLION Dollar Plan to Crush TSMC
videos.summary_source_text:
TERAFAB Explained: Elon's 119 BILLION Dollar Plan to Crush TSMC Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Elon Musk just announced TERAFAB — a $119,000,000,000 vertically integrated 
chip fab in Texas that's supposed to crush TSMC, save Intel, and power every 
Tesla, robot, xAI cluster, and Starshield satellite he ever builds.

But here's the truth: most of what you're reading about Terafab online is WRONG.

I went through every Tesla earnings call, every Reuters report, every SpaceX 
job listing, and the official Grimes County tax-abatement filings — and the 
real story is way more interesting than the hype.

In this video, I break down EXACTLY:

✅ What Terafab actually is (and what it isn't)
✅ The $3B research fab Musk confirmed on the April 2026 earnings call
✅ Why Intel's 14A process is the secret weapon nobody's talking about
✅ Tesla's AI5 & AI6 chip roadmap (50x more powerful than AI4)
✅ The xAI Colossus 1,000,000 GPU plan that's driving demand
✅ The REAL semiconductor war: it's not lithography — it's packaging
✅ The 10-gigawatt power problem that could kill the whole thing
✅ Best case, base case, and worst case for Terafab's future
✅ Who wins, who loses, and what it means for American AI

By the end, you'll know more about Terafab than 90% of the people 
arguing about it online.

⏱️ CHAPTERS
00:00 The $119B Story Everyone's Getting Wrong
01:15 What Terafab Actually Is
02:23 Where The Idea Came From
03:24 Why Musk Thinks He Needs This
04:49 The Reality Check Nobody Wants To Hear

07:49 The Hidden Battle Nobody Talks About
08:57 Putting The Hype Next To Reality
09:42 Location, Money & The Texas Factor
10:38 The National Security Layer
11:40 The 10-Gigawatt Power Problem
12:35 Who Wins If This Works
13:47 My Honest Base Case
15:16 The One-Sentence Takeaway

💬 COMMENT BELOW:
Is Terafab the start of an American AI-industrial moonshot, 
or is Musk overreaching into the hardest manufacturing problem on Earth?

🔔 SUBSCRIBE for deep dives on semiconductors, AI infrastructure, 
and the power politics behind the AI race — the next phase of this 
story is unfolding right now.

videos.title: How To Score From Awkward Positions
videos.summary_source_text:
How To Score From Awkward Positions The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

videos.title: Her Driver Went From Good to Incredible (One Lesson)
videos.summary_source_text:
Her Driver Went From Good to Incredible (One Lesson) 🏌️‍♂️ Clubhouse Golf – https://bit.ly/4eBgyeH

In this video I’m joined by Eloise Feely for a live driver lesson — and what happened surprised both of us.

Eloise was already hitting some good drives, but the miss was creeping too far to the right and the strike and distance weren’t as consistent as they could be.

With just a couple of key adjustments, everything started to change.

Her driver became:

more consistent
straighter
and noticeably longer

The best part? The change was actually much simpler than most golfers think.

If you struggle with driver accuracy or feel like your good drives don’t happen often enough, the concepts in this lesson could help you immediately.

In this video you’ll learn:
• Why many golfers miss the driver to the right
• The key movement that improved Eloise’s driver instantly
• How to create more consistent, powerful drives

Sometimes the biggest improvements come from the simplest changes.

videos.title: Why You Don’t Need So Many Swing Thoughts #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
Why You Don’t Need So Many Swing Thoughts #golfswing #golftips Good iron compression comes from reaching a strong impact position — with the body moving toward the target, lag maintained, and the clubface squaring naturally. While all of these matter, trying to focus on everything during one swing often leads to worse contact. The key is identifying the one piece your swing needs most and committing to that.
If you’re unsure what that priority is, head to the link in our bio. Our coaching plans give you a clear structure for iron play and every part of your game, helping you focus on what actually moves the needle. videos.title: 85% of Golfers Never Improve Because They're Not Taught This
videos.summary_source_text:
85% of Golfers Never Improve Because They're Not Taught This There is one ingredient in every good golf swing that successful players take completely for granted — and almost nobody ever teaches it. If you've been working hard on your golf swing, your body rotation, your shoulder turn, your hip action — and it still doesn't feel right — this is almost certainly what's missing.

Most golf swing tips focus on body positions and mechanics. But here's what the best players know that most golfers never discover: the hands and arms lead the golf swing. The body supports them. Not the other way around. When you know what your hands and arms are supposed to do — when you have what I call educated hands — everything else in your golf swing starts to organise itself naturally. The body stops making funky compensations because it finally has something to follow.

In this golf swing lesson I want to share the drill my mentor Graeme Walker gave me personally — one of the simplest and most effective ways to educate your hands and arms I have ever come across. Once you feel it, you'll understand immediately why so many golfers struggle despite working hard on their swing. And you'll wonder why nobody ever showed you this before.

⬇️ FREE PRACTICE GUIDE — https://www.dannymaude.com/golfers-arent-taught-this

What you'll learn in this golf swing lesson:
▶ The one ingredient every good golf swing has — and most golfers never develop ▶ Why working on your body without educated hands keeps you permanently stuck ▶ The lever drill that teaches your hands exactly what to do in the backswing ▶ How educated hands naturally square the club face without any extra effort ▶ Why your body starts to support your golf swing automatically once your hands lead ▶ How this golf swing feel works for hooks, slices and every ball flight problem ▶ Why great players take this for granted — and why that's kept it hidden from you

⏱️ CHAPTERS
0:00 — The one ingredient missing from most golf swings 1:15 — What educated hands actually means 2:00 — The hammer analogy — why hands lead everything 2:45 — The lever drill — backswing education 4:30 — The downswing — how educated hands close the face naturally 6:00 — Why this fixes hooks, slices and poor contact simultaneously 7:30 — The full flowing motion — putting educated hands together 9:45 — Timing your body with your educated hands 11:30 — How this changes everything you feel on the golf course

This golf swing lesson works for every golfer at every level — whether you're a beginner trying to understand why your golf swing never feels coordinated, a mid-handicapper who has spent years working on body mechanics without improvement, or an experienced golfer who occasionally loses the feel and can't find it again. If your golf swing has ever felt disconnected, mechanical or out of sequence — this is the missing piece. Not another position to remember. Not another body drill to practise. Just one simple feeling in your hands and arms that makes your entire golf swing make sense for the first time.

If this helped your golf swing, hit the like button and subscribe — I release a new golf lesson every single week to help you play better golf.

📩 Want personalised help with your golf swing? Head to https://www.dannymaude.com for lessons and coaching.

videos.title: What Your Hands Are Warning You About Your Heart
videos.summary_source_text:
What Your Hands Are Warning You About Your Heart Almost half of all heart attacks are silent heart attacks, which means there are no warning signs. Fortunately, your hands can reveal heart disease warning signs that could save your life. Discover the key health markers, like weak grip strength or clubbed fingers, that can provide important insight into your cardiovascular health.

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements.

🌟10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out: https://drbrg.co/4wAnHTd

0:00 Introduction: Hands and cardiovascular health
0:18 Heart attack symptoms
0:36 Grip strength test 
3:17 Clubbed fingers and white nails
7:40 Heart health tips  

Keep watching to discover more hidden warning signs and natural ways to support heart health:
▶️ https://youtu.be/uu3N_mSwJHw
▶️ https://youtu.be/HAK5eg0HRc8
▶️ https://youtu.be/hq9Al6LR2O4

Make a fist! It may tell you more about your heart health than blood pressure. Nearly 45–50% of heart attacks occur without warning symptoms, making early health markers especially important in heart attack prevention.

Here are 7 ways your hands may reveal early signs of heart disease and overall cardiovascular health:
• Weak grip strength
• Inability to lift the ring finger from a table
• Clubbed fingernails
• White nails
• Yellow bumps on the tendons of the hands
• Fine tremors
• Cold hands

These physical signs may provide insight into circulation, cardiovascular health, high cholesterol symptoms, and other underlying health conditions.

An A1C test, a resting heart rate test, and a fasting insulin test can also provide helpful insight into your cardiovascular health.

videos.title: Rick Shiels' Scotty Cameron collection 👀
videos.summary_source_text:
Rick Shiels' Scotty Cameron collection 👀 Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.

videos.title: FIRST EVER Lambo Temerario v Revuelto DRAG RACE
videos.summary_source_text:
FIRST EVER Lambo Temerario v Revuelto DRAG RACE Huge thanks to everyone who helped us set up this race:
- Lamborghini Revuelto: https://www.instagram.com/seanysgarage/ 
- Lamborghini Temerario: https://www.instagram.com/affluent_time/ 
- Lamborghini Temerario: https://www.instagram.com/joseph_sewell_/
- Lamborghini Temerario: https://www.youtube.com/@AffluentTime
- Lamborghini Temerario: https://affluent-time.co.uk/

We’ve got our hands on the Lamborghini Temerario, and it’s going up its older brother, the Revuelto! 

It’s Lamborghini’s replacement for the V10-powered Huracan, but does it have what it takes to go toe-to-toe with the Revuelto? Let’s check out the stats…

The Temerario is powered by a 4-litre twin-turbo V8, coupled with three electric motors. Combined, they produce an almighty 920hp and 730Nm, and it tips the scales at 1,790kg. As new, it costs £259,567. 

As for the Revuelto, it also has three electric motors, but there’s no V8 - instead, it’s housing a 6.5-litre naturally-aspirated V12! The engine and motors combine for 1,015hp and 1,062Nm, and it’s slightly heavier than the Temerario, at 1,872kg. It’s also a whole lot more expensive, costing £446,742. 

So on paper, it should be a clear win for the Revuelto, right? Well, there’s only one way to find out for sure… LET’S RACE! 

Explore new & used cars with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-16-05

Lamborghinis vs EVs: https://bit.ly/Carwow-Drag-Race-1605 Huge thanks to everyone who helped us set up this race:
